data.title: ECB Considers Raising Reserve Requirements to Ease Loss Pressure
data.description: The European Central Bank is considering several options to ease pressure from its financial losses, and the related discussions are expected to reach a key stage this autumn. According to Jin10, ECB President Christine Lagarde said policymakers will discuss raising minimum reserve requirements, which would increase the share of commercial banks' funds held in the central bank's non-interest-bearing accounts. The ECB is also evaluating a tiered interest-rate mechanism that would stop paying interest on some excess reserves, as well as charging banks fees. These measures are intended to reduce the burden on national central banks and offset part of the losses from stimulus policies over the past decade. The euro system has faced loss pressure after the ECB carried out a large-scale bond-buying program from 2015 to 2022 and paid banks substantial interest after rapid rate hikes from 2022 to 2023. According to Jin10, sources said there are still major differences over this politically sensitive issue.

data.title: STOCKS | Miami to Sell Griffin Historic Site Across From Planned Citadel HQ
data.description:
Miami has agreed to sell Ken Griffin a site across the street from Citadel’s planned headquarters in the Brickell financial district, completing his goal of owning the entire square block, according to Bloomberg.

The parcel currently houses an historic cottage.

data.title: Tassat Unveils Project NENYA for Stablecoin Reserve Management
data.description:
Tassat unveiled Project NENYA, a stablecoin reserve management platform designed for regional and midsize U.S. banks, as the sector is projected to grow into a multi-trillion-dollar market. According to NS3.AI, the platform is expected to help regulated stablecoin issuers allocate reserves across banks and tokenized high-quality liquid assets.

The company said pilot programs are set to begin in the first half of the year, with a full launch planned for early 2027. Tassat said the platform is aimed at giving banks and issuers a regulated framework for managing stablecoin reserves.

data.title: Hyperliquid Generates $1.19 Million in Fees and Burns 17,600 HYPE
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X that Hyperliquid generated $1.19 million in fee revenue today and burned 17,600 HYPE tokens worth $1.03 million. According to Odaily, the post said the tokens were destroyed as part of the activity reported for the day.

data.title: Tesla Short Sellers Book $4 Billion as Shares Plunge
data.description: Tesla Inc.'s sharp stock drop on Thursday has generated about $4 billion in profits for short sellers, according to Bloomberg. The move marks a major payday for investors betting against the electric-vehicle maker as its shares nosedived.

data.title: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X Account Was Hacked
data.description: Onchain Lens said on X that Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev's X account was hacked. According to Odaily, the post did not provide further details.

data.title: Trump Nears Decision On Iran Strike As Brent Tops $100
data.description:
Oil prices jumped Thursday after President Donald Trump said he is close to ordering a massive strike on Iran, with Brent crude topping $100 a barrel. According to BeInCrypto, Trump told Axios he has not made a final decision, but said everything is ready and any new attack would be bigger than the last US campaign, Operation Epic Fury.

Bitcoin fell about 2% to near $64,755 as traders moved defensively amid rising Middle East tensions.

data.title: STOCKS | Cerebras Shares Rise More Than 10% After AMD Partnership on AI Inference Platform
data.description: Cerebras shares rose more than 10% after AMD partnered with it to develop an AI inference platform for integrated chips. According to Jin10, the two companies are working together on the platform.

data.title: STOCKS | Microsoft Reports Intermittent Azure Connectivity and Latency Issues in West US Region
data.description: Microsoft (MSFT.O) said Azure's West US region has had intermittent connectivity outages and latency issues since 14:44 UTC on July 23. According to Jin10, the company is actively investigating network connectivity and traffic routing anomalies related to the start time of the impact.

data.title: AI TRENDS | AMD and Cerebras Systems to Launch AI Inference Solution
data.description: According to Jin10, AMD and Cerebras Systems will launch an AI inference solution combining AMD Helios GPU server racks with Cerebras wafer-scale chips.

data.title: MoonPay Adds Discover Network to U.S. Crypto Buy and Sell Options
data.description: MoonPay has added Discover Network to its U.S. cryptocurrency buy and sell options, expanding support to all three major U.S. card networks. According to NS3.AI, MoonPay already supported Visa and Mastercard. The company said existing payment methods should continue to work the same way for crypto transactions.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Say They Carried Out Previous Attacks on Kuwait's Adaili Camp and U.S. Troops Station
data.description: According to Jin10, Iran's Revolutionary Guards said it had previously launched attacks on Kuwait's Adaili camp and a U.S. military station.

data.title: Michelle Steel Hails U.S.-South Korea Shipbuilding Cooperation As 'Top Priority'
data.description:
According to Yonhap, new U.S. Ambassador to South Korea Michelle Steel said South Korea's shipbuilding cooperation with the United States shows the alliance is expanding into a new and important area, and she called strengthening the partnership her top priority. Steel made the remarks at the opening of the Korea-U.S. Shipbuilding Partnership Center in Washington, which is meant to support the US$150 billion investment South Korea has pledged for the U.S. shipbuilding sector under last year's bilateral trade and investment deal.

She cited Hanwha Group's $5 billion infrastructure plan for a shipyard in Philadelphia, HD Hyundai's multi-billion-dollar investment fund with Cerberus Capital Management, and Samsung Heavy Industries' strategic partnership with Vigor Marine Group. Steel also said the shipbuilding effort reflects a broader vision for a fair, reciprocal and resilient trade relationship between the two countries. She told Yonhap that she will depart for South Korea next week and may take up her post in Seoul as early as late this month.

data.title: Honeywell Technologies Shares Rally After Strong Quarter, Raised Outlook
data.description: According to CNBC, Honeywell Technologies shares rose Thursday after the industrial automation company reported second-quarter revenue of $9.72 billion, up 4.3% from a year earlier and above the LSEG consensus estimate of $9.5 billion, and raised its guidance. Adjusted earnings per share fell 4.2% to $4.52, below the LSEG estimate of $4.27. The results still include Honeywell Aerospace, which was spun off into a separate publicly traded company at the end of the quarter. Excluding aerospace, total orders increased 16% organically, backlog grew 9%, and management lifted its outlook for organic growth, segment margin and adjusted EPS for the full year. Honeywell also guided third-quarter sales to $4.9 billion to $5 billion and adjusted EPS to $2.05 to $2.20, while fourth-quarter sales were projected at $5 billion to $5.1 billion and adjusted EPS at $2.28 to $2.43.

data.title: Fazenda Engenho Velho Uses Ten Cows to Secure R$100,000 CPR-F Loan on B3
data.description:
Fazenda Engenho Velho used ten cows to secure a R$100,000 CPR-F loan registered on B3. According to NS3.AI, each cow received a unique blockchain ID based on Cowmed sensor data.

The model is designed to help reduce livestock collateral discounts for farmers by providing more detailed identification and tracking of the animals used as loan security.

data.title: Iranian Media Says Tanker Enters the Sea of Oman Despite Claimed U.S. Blockade
data.description: According to Jin10, Tasnim News Agency reported that a tanker belonging to Iran's fleet ignored the U.S.-claimed blockade and entered the Sea of Oman from the high seas a few minutes ago.

data.title: AMD CEO Su Sees Collaboration With Cerebras on High-Speed Inference Services
data.description: AMD CEO Lisa Su said the company is working with chip design company Cerebras to provide high-speed inference through Cerebras' cloud services. According to Jin10, she made the remarks about the collaboration.

data.title: Israel's Defense Minister Katz: Iran Will Suffer a Devastating Blow If It Attacks Israel
data.description: According to Jin10, Israel's Defense Minister Katz said Iran will suffer a devastating blow if it attacks Israel.

data.title: AMD CEO Lisa Su Says New Server CPU Has Clear Advantage Over Arm-Based Chips
data.description: According to Jin10, AMD CEO Lisa Su said the company’s new server CPU has a clear lead over Arm-based chips.

data.title: AMD CEO Lisa Su Says New EPYC Processor Offers 20% Better CPU Performance Than Nvidia
data.description: According to Jin10, AMD CEO Lisa Su said the new EPYC processor delivers 20% better CPU performance than Nvidia.
